content: LMS-embed Part 2 (from sovern-mindmap, slice 7 done): embed a Learn-mode walkthrough HTML into a course unit. sovern-mindmap now has Export Learn HTML — a self-contained interactive step-through file (cumulative node reveal + per-step narration + prev/next + keyboard, neutral hybrid background that reads on both light and dark pages, zero external resources). Manual one-shot authoring flow: (1) author builds a small diagram in the mindmap app and clicks Export Learn HTML; (2) drop the exported <slug>.html into LMS/tochka-sborki/web/public/walkthroughs/; (3) embed it via a sandboxed <iframe> in a unit — mirror the LearnWithAI dock injection point in unit-wizard.tsx (fixed aspect/min-height, lazy-load). No code dependency on the app: the file is fully self-contained and keeps working if the mindmap app goes away. Keep walkthrough diagrams SMALL (~5 nodes): cumulative inline-SVG frames get heavy (~22MB for 13 nodes). Gotcha: static asset + iframe ONLY — do NOT pull shared sources into workers/ (relative-import / @-alias tsc trap from monorepo memory). Optional follow-up: a walkthrough could also become a showcase-gallery item on home.
title: LMS-embed: Walkthrough-компонент для self-contained Learn-mode HTML (sovern-mindmap)

content: Content-design principles (pedagogy) baked into the LMS engine and the AI-authoring pipeline: (1) Curse of Knowledge — experts forget what beginners don't know (Piaget); design lessons from the beginner's vantage, show-the-menu-before-asking. (2) Cognitive overload prevention via Must/Should/Could-Know (MoSCoW) prioritization of every lesson's content. (3) Andragogy — adult-learner attributes (self-directed, experience-anchored, problem-centered, intrinsically motivated). Reverse-engineered from Google's Facilitation Bootcamp deck. Feed these as guardrails/checklist into the AI course-authoring engine (fb_8e8eaf0a) and as a review-pass lens; also reinforces the funnel 'show the menu' thesis.
title: Content-design principles: curse-of-knowledge, Must/Should/Could prioritization, andragogy

triage.reason: Принципы дизайна контента из Google Facilitation Bootcamp → guardrails движка/авторинга: curse-of-knowledge (Piaget — проектировать с позиции новичка, 'показать меню'), Must/Should/Could (MoSCoW против когнитивной перегрузки), андрагогика. Питают авторинг-движок fb_8e8eaf0a как чеклист/ревью-линза; усиливают funnel-тезис 'показать меню'. Engine: применимо ко всем курсам · LIVE-ВАЛИДАЦИЯ 2026-06-19 (Наташа): «у тебя Аспергер → ты думаешь, если тебе понятно, значит всем понятно. content: Anti-dependency pedagogy principle for the LMS engine + authoring engine: design the course to GRADUATE the learner into an autonomous practitioner/teacher, not to retain them as a perpetual student. De-guru'd from Yogi Bhajan's 'a teacher makes a teacher; never control the student; teach them to fish; a student is inlet and outlet.' Concretely: optimize for learner agency and exit-velocity, avoid retention/dependency dark-patterns, and frame progression as free course -> capable builder -> S.A.S.H.A admission -> mentor/community contributor. Companion reflection principle: 'the chance to elevate is lost when you are in reaction' (respond, don't react) -> candidate wellbeing/reflection nudge. Feeds the authoring engine (fb_8e8eaf0a) and the funnel.
title: Anti-dependency pedagogy: design for graduation/agency, not retention (teach-to-fish)

triage.reason: Де-гуру'нуто из YB 'учитель делает учителя, teach-to-fish, never control': движок/авторинг оптимизируют на ВЫПУСК/агентность, не удержание; против retention dark-patterns; прогрессия free→билдер→S.A.S.H.A→ментор (совпадает с воронкой и gran-нарративом fb_e0529c53). LLMs are sycophantic by default; the companion/mentor (learn-with-AI fb_e60901, Telegram bot fb_5e4afe37) should support without flattering, hold the standard, and push back honestly rather than validate everything. Pairs with the facilitation playbook archetypes (fb_c3471241) and the intake 'confronting AI / pushing back logically' option (fb_8f119f). content: Kolb experiential learning cycle as the lesson-phase backbone for the LMS/authoring engine. Reverse-engineered from the 'Узкое место наставника' infographic (de-funnel'd — drop its Telegram-channel CTA / mentoring sales framing). Adults absorb knowledge only by passing 4 stages: (1) Concrete Experience -> (2) Reflective Observation -> (3) Abstract Conceptualization (models/rules) -> (4) Active Experimentation. Guardrail: do NOT front-load content before the learner engages their own experience + reflection (stages 1-2), or retention and transfer-to-practice collapse. This is the theoretical backbone for what we already have — lesson phases activation (concrete-experience hook) -> reflection (bisociative, mental, drift-guarded) -> content (conceptualization) -> EXERCISES (experimentation). Formalize the cycle as an authoring/review checklist so every lesson covers all 4 stages. Also: 'if content doesn't land, it's a teaching-technology issue, not a broken learner' — reinforces the warm-but-firm anti-blame mentor (fb_e3d6506231a8). Pairs with content-design principles (fb_cea25e03).
